channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Why Dan Campbell believes Detroit Lions' Super Bowl window is still open
item.description: Dan Campbell explained why he still believes the Detroit Lions have a window to win the Super Bowl after their devastating loss to the Commanders.
item.dc:creator: Derick Hutchinson, Local 4

item.title: Spurs, Pacers make their way to Paris, for a week where all eyes will be on Victor Wembanyama
item.description: NANTERRE, France (AP) — Officially, Indiana will be the home team on the scoreboard when it plays San Antonio in Paris on Thursday. The Pacers know better. They will not be feeling right at home. “It’s going to be a big crowd against us, honestly,” Indiana guard Andrew Nembhard said.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Former American Olympian, who is lifelong Democrat, changes tune on Trump over key policy issue
item.description: Former American Olympian Inga Thompson admitted she changed her tune on Donald Trump over his commitment to ban trans inclusion in women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: There's a race this weekend, so Justin Marks made sure Trackhouse Racing had entry in the field
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Justin Marks had grandiose ideas when he bought Chip Ganassi Racing ahead of the 2022 NASCAR season. The former driver is probably the most forward-thinking team owner in NASCAR and in three seasons is making his mark all over motorsports.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: UFC star Conor McGregor 'very optimistic' ahead of Trump's presidency
item.description: UFC star Conor McGregor expressed optimism for the future as Donald Trump is set to retake the White House as the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hail to the Chiefs? The most despised team in the Bay Area nears another Super Bowl
item.description: It's up to Buffalo, Philadelphia or Washington to halt the Chiefs in their drive to win three straight Super Bowls.
item.dc:creator: Jerry McDonald, The Mercury News

item.title: Novak Djokovic gets his apologies and is ready to focus on Carlos Alcaraz at the Australian Open
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Novak Djokovic got the mea culpas he wanted from the Australian Open's local broadcaster and its employee who insulted him on the air and so, according to tournament organizers, the 24-time Grand Slam champion is ready to concentrate on his quarterfinal showdown with Carl
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jannik Sinner gets past Holger Rune at the Australian Open in a match with net and medical delays
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — First came the medical timeouts, one each for Jannik Sinner and Holger Rune with the temperature above 90 degrees Fahrenheit (32 Celsius) at the Australian Open.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ichiro is about to get his Hall of Fame moment. For Japan, he's more than just a baseball star
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Ichiro Suzuki is all about baseball, but he's much more than that in Japan. Back home, he's a wellspring of national pride, much like Shohei Ohtani now.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WADE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Josh Allen scores 2 TDs, Bills force 3 turnovers to beat Ravens 27-25 and reach AFC title game vs. Chiefs
item.description: Josh Allen heard the “MVP! MVP!” chants directed at him from the stands, but Buffalo's quarterback deflected the credit following the Bills' hard-fought victory in the divisional playoff round.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Broadcaster apologizes after Novak Djokovic refuses post-match TV interview at the Australian Open
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — The host broadcaster has apologized after Novak Djokovic declined to do a customary post-match on court interview at the Australian Open Sunday to protest comments made on air by someone who works for the TV network.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Josh Allen scores 2 TDs, Bills force 3 turnovers to beat Ravens 27-25 and reach AFC title game
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Josh Allen heard the “MVP! MVP!” chants directed at him from the stands, but Buffalo's quarterback deflected the credit following the Bills' hard-fought victory in the divisional playoff round. This season's Bills were thought to be thin on both sides of the ball.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Powell and the Clippers beat James and the Lakers 116-102 in Lakers' first visit to Intuit Dome
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — Norman Powell scored 22 points, James Harden added 21 points and 12 assists and the Los Angeles Clippers defeated LeBron James and the Los Angeles Lakers 116-102 on Sunday night in the Lakers' first visit to the new Intuit Dome.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jeff Torborg, former big league catcher and manager, dies at 83
item.description: Jeff Torborg, the former catcher who caught Sandy Koufax's perfect game and was the 1990 AL manager of the year with the Chicago White Sox, died Sunday. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Josh Allen scores 2 touchdowns, Bills force 3 turnovers in divisional round win over Ravens
item.description: The Buffalo Bills forced three turnovers and Josh Allen scored two rushing touchdowns to defeat the Baltimore Ravens and advance to the AFC title game.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jeff Torborg, World Series champ and former MLB manager, dead at 83
item.description: Jeff Torborg, a former MLB catcher and manager, has died, the Chicago White Sox announced on Sunday. The 83-year-old played 10 seasons in the majors and managed 11 more.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bills will face Chiefs in AFC title game; Eagles will host the Commanders in NFC championship game
item.description: Josh Allen and the Buffalo Bills will take on Patrick Mahomes and the Kansas City Chiefs in the AFC championship game in their fourth playoff matchup in five years.
item.dc:creator: Rob Maaddi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Comeback attempt falls short, Rams lose to Eagles in NFL Divisional Round
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Saquon Barkley dashed through the snow for touchdown runs of 78 and 62 yards and finished with 205 yards rushing, Jalen Hurts had a 44-yard scoring run and the Philadelphia Eagles held on against the turnover-happy Los Angeles Rams 28-22 on Sunday to advance to the NFC championsh
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Brittany Mahomes stuns fans with Chiefs playoff game appearance days after giving birth
item.description: Brittany Mahomes, the wife of Kansas City Chiefs star Patrick Mahomes, was praised for showing up at the team's playoff game days after giving birth.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coco Gauff writes 'RIP TikTok USA' on a TV camera at the Australian Open
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — American tennis star Coco Gauff mourned the loss of TikTok's app back home, writing on a TV camera lens “RIP TikTok USA” and drawing a broken heart right after winning a match at the Australian Open to reach the quarterfinals.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dodgers continue offseason dominance, sign All-Star reliever
item.description: As the Los Angeles Dodgers stormed toward the World Series last October, one weakness was consistently brought up for the Boys in Blue: relief pitching.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: NFL refs under fire over questionable calls in Chiefs' playoff win over Texans
item.description: NFL officials calling the Kansas City Chiefs-Houston Texans playoff game on Saturday faced tremendous criticism from fans, players and broadcasters alike.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coco Gauff drops a set but reaches the Australian Open quarterfinals. Sabalenka wins, too
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Coco Gauff's consecutive-set streak ended at the Australian Open. Her bid for a second Grand Slam title continued on Sunday with a 5-7, 6-2, 6-1 comeback victory over Belinda Bencic in the fourth round.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Travis Kelce makes NFL history in first game of three-peat quest
item.description: Once again, Travis Kelce has broken a record at Arrowhead Stadium after catching a pass from Patrick Mahomes.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, KTLA 5

item.title: Turnovers and depleted defense contribute to top-seeded Lions' quick playoff exit
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The Detroit Lions had everything set up the way they wanted this time. After losing last season's NFC championship game at San Francisco, they went 15-2 to earn the NFC's top seed.
item.dc:creator: DAVE HOGG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jayden Daniels and the Commanders stun the top-seeded Lions 45-31 to reach NFC title game
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Jayden Daniels and the nothing-to-lose Washington Commanders sent the top-seeded Detroit Lions to a stunningly swift playoff exit.
item.dc:creator: LARRY LAGE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texans' Kris Boyd flings helmet, gets penalized and then shoves his coach on first play vs. Chiefs
item.description: Texans cornerback Kris Boyd nearly shoved Houston special teams coach Frank Ross to the ground after the opening kickoff of their divisional playoff loss to the Kansas City Chiefs on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mahomes and Kelce help Chiefs to a 23-14 win over Texans and another AFC title game trip
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Patrick Mahomes has never lost in the divisional round of the playoffs with the Kansas City Chiefs. The Houston Texans still have never won.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Washington retires its first women's jersey, sending Kelsey Plum's No. 10 to the rafters
item.description: It was always a matter of when — not if — Washington would retire Kelsey Plum’s number. Eight years after she graduated, it happened.
item.dc:creator: JOSH KIRSHENBAUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: San Diego FC players react to first week of practice
item.description: The "Chrome & Azul" are back on the training ground to finish off their first week of training, for the first time ever as a club.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Neff injured in Red Rocks win over Iowa State
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The #4 Utah gymnastics team came away with an easy victory over Iowa State in its Big 12 Conference opener Friday night, but it came at a price.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Chiefs fans banking on three-peat during Wild Card Weekend
item.description: The NFL playoffs kicked off on Saturday, but the Kansas City Chiefs have to wait another week for their playoff campaign to start. It’s leaving fans with mixed feelings.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Sloan, NewsNation

item.title: Mojo fan gifts head coach handmade jacket
item.description: “Aside from an Olympic medal, and we had to earn that one, this is probably one of the best gifts I've ever received,” San Diego Mojo head coach Tayyiba Haneef-Park said.
item.dc:creator: Frank Jefferson, Fox 5

item.title: Irish eyes smile on new path for Silver Pigskin winner Buchner
item.description: For 2019 Silver Pigskin winner Tyler Buchner, the end of the path lands in the same place as its start - back in South Bend, playing with Notre Dame and seeking a national championship. The journey includes a new position, a new sport, and a dip in the SEC.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Stone, Fox 5

item.title: Several Chicagoland AMC locations to air the College Football Playoff championship game
item.description: CHICAGO — Notre Dame will face off against Ohio State on Monday in the College Football Playoff championship game in Atlanta, and while traveling to the game may not be an option for some, fans in Chicago can still get a taste of the larger-than-life action at some AMC theaters.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, WGN 9

item.title: Australian Open 2025: How to watch on TV, betting odds and more to know
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Get caught up on the Australian Open with a guide that tells you everything you need to know about how to watch the year’s first Grand Slam tennis tournament, what the betting odds are, what the schedule is and more: How to watch the Australian Open on TV — In the
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utah Valley wins on overtime buzzer-beater, 85-83
item.description: ARLINGTON, Tex. (ABC4 Sports) - Texas native Kylin Green went the full length of the floor in five seconds and dished to Jackson Holcombe, who scored in the final second of overtime to lift Utah Valley to a thrilling 85-83 road victory over UT Arlington on Thursday night at College Park Center.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
